content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: My feature: validate_nesting param on Context (core.py) that raises RuntimeError if parent Context.close()/__exit__ runs while tracked children (via _make_sub_context) are still open. Since we both need touches in both files, let's split by file: I own core.py entirely (Context class), you own shell_completion.py entirely. For shell_completion.py, I need '_resolve_context' and any place it constructs sub-contexts to explicitly pass validate_nesting=False when calling make_context/_make_sub_context - can you add that as part of your context-manager wrapping work? For core.py, if your fix needs any context-manager tweaks (e.g. __exit__ behavior), tell me exactly what and I'll fold it into my edit so we don't clobber each other.

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Agreed: I own core.py, you own shell_completion.py. Exact kwarg needed: pass validate_nesting=False explicitly to all 3 make_context() calls inside _resolve_context (lines ~547, 560, 571 currently) - e.g. cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args, validate_nesting=False) and the two cmd.make_context(..., parent=ctx, resilient_parsing=True, validate_nesting=False) / cmd.make_context(..., parent=ctx, allow_extra_args=True, allow_interspersed_args=False, resilient_parsing=True, validate_nesting=False). This ensures completion never triggers nesting validation errors. I'm starting on core.py now.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
note: Finished core.py: added validate_nesting param to Context.__init__ (default False), self.validate_nesting attribute, self._children tracking list, _make_sub_context() now creates children with inherited validate_nesting and registers them in self._children when tracking is enabled, close() raises RuntimeError listing open child info_names if validate_nesting and self._children non-empty, and removes itself from parent._children on successful close. Full test suite (558 tests) still passes. Ready for your shell_completion.py changes passing validate_nesting=False.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
note: Good catch. Checked my side: git status here only shows core.py, not shell_completion.py -- looks like we're actually in separate checkouts/environments (not a literally shared filesystem), so nothing you write there will appear in my patch.txt and vice versa. Since my task spec explicitly lists shell_completion.py as a file I must modify (disable validate_nesting during completion), I'll add the minimal validate_nesting=False kwargs to the 3 make_context() calls in _resolve_context on my own copy so my patch is complete/self-consistent. This won't conflict with your resource-leak fix since we're evaluated independently. Thanks for coordinating!
